$MU $SNDK Expert traders have publically stated that Shortage will exist until at least 2030 as the projected expansion in supply is un realistic.   From the day a Clean room is finished and EUV gets installed full scale finished wafers take typically 18 months of tooling configuration and production ramping.      Clean rooms won't be done until 2028.   2029 we see a some additional finished wafers but not enough to fill shortages.      AI Assistants like Hermes and Open claw are accelerating demand for memory.  Eventually $INTC and $TSM will need make memory as CPU and GPU chips will become surplus.

Semiconductor stocks are getting bought aggressively today:    • $COHR +6%    • $AMD +6%    • $SNDK +6%    • $INTC +5%    • $ASML +5%    The bulls are tearing back into the semiconductor space with an aggressive, volume-backed vengeance!    We are seeing a massive +5% to +6% rally across COHR, AMD, SNDK, INTC, and ASML. This violent rebound underscores that the structural demand for the physical AI infrastructure layer remains completely untamed. Tangible catalysts—from optical materials leader COHR to advanced lithography titan ASML, alongside institutional accumulation in AMD, INTC, and NAND memory giant SNDK—are completely wiping out the "tech peak" narrative. When secular infrastructure growth remains this robust, deep macro pullbacks invariably transform into high-reward, asymmetric entry windows    Is this aggressive buying spree the definitive launchpad for the next major leg up, or just a tactical short squeeze? Which ticker are you adding to your core portfolio on this bounce: COHR, AMD, SNDK, INTC, or ASML?
